name: Work-Stealing Scheduler
def: A scheduler where idle worker threads steal tasks from busy workers’ queues to balance load.
problem: Keep all cores busy on irregular, dynamically-generated task graphs without central bottlenecks.
mechanism: Each worker has a double-ended queue; it pushes/pops its own end (LIFO, cache-warm) and steals from the other end of victims when idle.
limits: Poor for fine-grained or imbalanced tasks
failures: Task granularity too small → overhead dominates
whenNot: Regular, evenly-divisible data parallelism (simple partitioning wins)
myths: "Always optimal" — overhead can exceed benefit for tiny tasks
